title: bauhinia khasiana subsp. polystachya
intro: Bauhinia khasiana subsp. polystachya, also known as the Khasi hill orchid tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to the Himalayas and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin infections, due to its ability to reduce inflammation, protect against oxidative stress, and exhibit antibacterial properties. Traditionally, Bauhinia khasiana subsp. polystachya is prepared as a decoction, powder, or paste, and is used in herbal medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
